repo_notes.content: bitcold is a TypeScript CLI for Bitcoin cold-wallet management, local encrypted wallet storage, recovery material handling, and offline transaction signing.

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce bitcold's product goal, offline-first workflow, major capabilities, and the top-level source layout.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Cover installation, build/test commands, CLI entrypoint, runtime environment variables, and first-run setup.

pages.title: Command Reference
pages.purpose: Map the full CLI command tree and summarize each command's arguments, options, prompts, and output.

pages.title: Architecture
pages.purpose: Explain the main layers and how data flows through CLI commands, domain objects, crypto helpers, and encrypted persistence.

pages.title: CLI Layer
pages.purpose: Describe command registration, command implementations, shared CLI utilities, output handling, and error handling.

pages.title: Domain Model
pages.purpose: Describe the wallet, account, address, and transaction domain objects and their responsibilities.

pages.title: Persistence
pages.purpose: Describe encrypted wallet storage, repository responsibilities, storage location, initialization, and CRUD flow.

pages.title: Wallet Workflows
pages.purpose: Group wallet lifecycle workflows: create, import, recover, show, list, rename, remove, receive, and account management.

pages.title: Wallet Creation and Recovery
pages.purpose: Cover wallet creation from generated mnemonic, imported mnemonic, custom entropy, and SLIP-39 recovery shares.

pages.title: Wallet Display and Receive
pages.purpose: Cover wallet show/list/receive behavior, address references, private data flags, entropy display, QR output, and change-chain receive addresses.

pages.title: Account Management
pages.purpose: Cover account add/remove workflows, account aliases, derivation indexes, and top-level account command shortcuts.

pages.title: Transaction Signing
pages.purpose: Cover tx sign workflow, input validation, transaction preview, confirmation, raw transaction output, and QR output.

pages.title: PSBT Construction
pages.purpose: Explain how UTXOs become a PSBT, how outputs/change are calculated, and how the final transaction is signed.

pages.title: Address References
pages.purpose: Explain wallet@account[:index] references, raw address validation, and how commands resolve local wallet references.

pages.title: Cryptography
pages.purpose: Organize the cryptographic subsystems: storage encryption, HD wallet derivation, mnemonic handling, and SLIP-39.

pages.title: Storage Encryption
pages.purpose: Cover AES-GCM encrypted envelopes, scrypt key derivation, and CLI passphrase based decryption.

pages.title: HD Wallet Derivation
pages.purpose: Cover BIP39 mnemonic handling, BIP32/BIP84 derivation paths, network-specific coin types, and account/address key derivation.

pages.title: SLIP-39
pages.purpose: Cover the supported SLIP-39 split/recover scope, command integration, and compatibility expectations.

pages.title: Security Model
pages.purpose: Explain the separation between CLI storage passphrase, optional BIP39 passphrase, sensitive outputs, and offline/air-gapped usage.

pages.title: Testing
pages.purpose: Describe unit and E2E test organization, CLI sandboxing, prompt automation, truth-set tests, and command workflow coverage.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.purpose: Define project-specific terms, command concepts, Bitcoin standards, and security-sensitive recovery/signing terminology.
